menu.subtitle: Digital Geopolitical Simulator
setup.neutralNote: Territories of inactive superpowers stay unclaimed — conquerable during the match.
setup.aiDifficultyNote: The AI plays by the same rules — only the quality of its decisions changes.
setup.marketModeNote: How prices and selling work in the match.
ai.diff.beginner.label: Beginner
ai.diff.beginner.hint: Plays simply, makes mistakes, little strategy.
ai.diff.intermediate.label: Intermediate
ai.diff.intermediate.hint: Plays correctly, defends and saves up.
ai.diff.advanced.label: Advanced
ai.diff.advanced.hint: Assesses threats and plans a few turns ahead.
ai.diff.god.label: God
market.balanced.label: Balanced Digital
market.balanced.hint: Simultaneous selling at the start of the round — reduces the first player's economic edge.
market.classic.label: Classic Grow
market.classic.hint: Faithful to Grow's basic mode. Market at $5,000 and selling in turn order.
phase.1.name: Wages
phase.2.name: Production
phase.3.name: Sell
phase.4.name: Attack
phase.5.name: Move
phase.6.name: Build
phase.7.name: Buy
tutorial.level.fullHint: Explanatory cards appear on their own during the first rounds.
tutorial.level.tipsHint: Just a short hint per phase.
tutorial.mandatory: Mandatory phase — it happens automatically and can't be skipped.
tutorial.intro.title: Welcome to Supremacy
tutorial.intro.whatFor: You command a superpower. The goal is to dominate the world by force (Supremacy) or by economy (Détente).
tutorial.intro.actions: Each round you go through phases: you collect wages and production, then choose up to 3 optional phases (sell, attack, move, build, buy).
tutorial.intro.strategy: Early on, focus on understanding your cards and territories. Resources (grain, oil, mineral) become money and military power.
tutorial.intro.ifIgnored: Win by controlling 12+ territories, or by having the most total wealth when Détente is declared. Too many nukes lead everyone to the Holocaust.
tutorial.phase.1.title: Wages Phase
tutorial.phase.1.whatFor: You pay upkeep for your troops. It's the first thing in the round.
tutorial.phase.1.actions: Tap “Wages” on the phase bar to pay automatically.
tutorial.phase.1.strategy: Always keep a cash reserve. Too many troops without cash becomes a loss.
tutorial.phase.1.ifIgnored: Can't be skipped — without money for wages, troops desert.
tutorial.phase.2.title: Production Phase
tutorial.phase.2.whatFor: Your territories and companies generate resources automatically.
tutorial.phase.2.actions: Tap “Production” to collect. Each active company produces its territory's resource.
tutorial.phase.2.strategy: The more company territories you control, the more you produce. Protect your production hubs.
tutorial.phase.2.ifIgnored: Can't be skipped — production is the base of your economy.
tutorial.phase.2.cost: No cost. Territories destroyed by a nuke produce nothing.
tutorial.phase.3.title: Sell Phase
tutorial.phase.3.whatFor: Turn resources into money on the market.
tutorial.phase.3.actions: Open the market at the bottom and sell grain, oil or mineral.
tutorial.phase.3.strategy: Prices drop as everyone sells. Sell when high and don't dump it all at once.
tutorial.phase.3.ifIgnored: Optional phase. Skipping is fine if you don't need money now.
tutorial.phase.3.cost: No cost — you earn money. On round 1 selling is simultaneous for everyone; from round 2 it follows normal turn order.
tutorial.phase.4.title: Attack Phase
tutorial.phase.4.actions: Tap a territory of yours with 2+ armies (1 always stays to defend) and pick the neighboring target.
tutorial.phase.4.strategy: Attack where you have a troop advantage. Conquering a territory where you hold a card lets you install a company and profit.
tutorial.phase.4.ifIgnored: Optional phase. Without a good reason, keeping troops and resources is usually safer.
tutorial.phase.4.cost: Each battle consumes 1 of each supply (grain, oil and mineral).
tutorial.phase.5.title: Movement Phase
tutorial.phase.5.whatFor: Reposition armies and ships for attack, defense or expansion.
tutorial.phase.5.actions: Tap a territory with troops and pick an adjacent destination you control. Ships move across connected seas.
tutorial.phase.5.strategy: Mass force near the right borders before attacking, or reinforce what's threatened.
tutorial.phase.5.ifIgnored: Optional phase. Skip it if your troops are already well placed.
tutorial.phase.6.title: Build Phase
tutorial.phase.6.whatFor: Create armies and ships, or research nukes and Laser-Stars.
tutorial.phase.6.actions: Open the build panel and choose what to produce in your territories. The cost shows before you confirm.
tutorial.phase.6.strategy: Armies defend and conquer; ships control seas; nukes and lasers change the game but cost a lot.
tutorial.phase.6.ifIgnored: Optional phase. Skip it to save up and grow the economy first.
tutorial.phase.6.cost: $1,000 + 1 supply set per 3 units. Researching tech costs more.
tutorial.phase.7.title: Buy Phase
tutorial.phase.7.whatFor: Buy resources on the market when you lack them to move, attack or build.
tutorial.phase.7.actions: Open the market at the bottom and buy the supply you need.
tutorial.phase.7.strategy: Buy when the price is low. Useful to close out an attack or build this turn.
tutorial.phase.7.ifIgnored: Optional phase. Skip it if you already have the supplies you need.
tutorial.phase.7.cost: You spend money based on each resource's current price.
tutorial.topic.territory.title: Territory Cards
tutorial.topic.territory.whatFor: Cards link a territory to a resource company.
tutorial.topic.territory.actions: Conquer the territory shown on your card to install the company and produce.
tutorial.topic.territory.strategy: Conquering a territory where you hold a card is worth more than any territory — it becomes recurring production.
tutorial.topic.territory.ifIgnored: Without the matching card, a conquered territory won't generate that resource for you.
tutorial.topic.seas.title: Seas and Ships
tutorial.topic.seas.whatFor: Ships control sea zones and project power across the sea.
tutorial.topic.seas.actions: Move ships across connected seas. Where the rule allows, fleets bombard coastal territories.
tutorial.topic.seas.strategy: Controlling seas isolates enemies and opens attack routes land can't reach.
tutorial.topic.seas.ifIgnored: Ignoring the sea leaves your coast exposed to bombardment and landings.
tutorial.rules.1: You have 2 mandatory phases (Wages + Production) and may use up to 3 of the 5 optional phases per round.
tutorial.rules.2: Supremacy victory: control 12+ territories. Détente victory: have the most total wealth when Détente is declared.
tutorial.rules.3: If too many territories are nuked, Nuclear Holocaust happens and everyone loses.
tutorial.rules.4: Laser-Stars can intercept nukes (50% chance per star).
tutorial.rules.5: The market is shared — when someone sells a lot, the price drops for everyone.
hud.companiesShow: See opportunities: highlights on the map the territories of your company cards
hud.ctxStart: You get {max} optional actions per turn, chosen in sequence (you can't go back to an earlier phase).
hud.ctxLimit: All {max} actions used — move on to the next player.
hud.ctxProgress: {ordinal} action done. You can still take {remain} — only phases ahead of the last one.
hud.pastPhase: Earlier phase — actions must be chosen in increasing order
hud.limitTitle: Limit of {max} actions reached this turn
hud.salaryShortTitle: Not enough wages for all companies
hud.salaryShortDesc: {count} company(ies) won't produce this turn: {names}. Sell resources or take a loan to pay and reactivate them next turn.
notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.body: Sorry, the page you are looking for doesn't exist. It may have been moved or deleted.
placement.title: Place your starting armies
landscape.title: Rotate your phone
landscape.body: The map looks much better in landscape. But you can play in portrait too.
landscape.iosHint: Turn off rotation lock on iOS (Control Center)
news.label: EVENTS
audio.title: Audio controls
nuke.title: Nuclear Strike
nuke.defenderHasLaser: Defender has a Laser-Star! Trying to intercept...
eventLog.title: Event Log
status.noFundsWarn: Without funds, units will be removed at the next payment.
combat.title: Combat
combat.originCleared: The attack's origin was left with no units.
combat.resisted: You held! Reinforce the territory and/or counter-attack the origin before ending.
market.title: Market / Commodities
market.subtitle: Price per unit. Change since the previous round. Updates on every buy/sell and new round.
market.historyStart: History starts at the end of round 1.
cards.noCompanies: No companies yet. Prospect or conquer territories with companies.
cards.producingNote: {producing} of {total} produce. Territories without a company generate no resource — prospect to find one.
prospect.title: Prospect Cards
prospect.payPerCard: Pay {cost} per card. Companies are discovered here — controlling a territory gives no production.
prospect.byResourceNote: Flips cards (charging {cost} each) until a company of the chosen resource appears. The rest go back to the deck.
sells.title: Simultaneous Sale
sells.intro: All players sell simultaneously. Everyone gets the current price. Then the market drops based on the total sold by all.
sells.usesAction: Selling ≥ 1 uses 1 of your 3 optional actions this round.
lobby.title: Online Multiplayer
lobby.localMode: Local test mode (same device, multiple tabs) — Supabase not configured.
lobby.subtitle: Play in rooms with friends and AIs.
lobby.waitingReady: Waiting for everyone to be ready…
bs.opp.own: You own a company here and control the territory.
bs.opp.neutral: You own a company here. Conquer the territory to maximize production.
bs.opp.enemy: You own a company here, but the territory belongs to another player.
bs.foreignCompany: There's a company here owned by another player. Conquer the territory to capture it.
bs.noCompanyMine: You control this territory, but don't have a resource company here yet.
bs.noCompanyKnown: No known active company in this territory.
bs.howToCompanyToast: Companies are obtained by prospecting (Buy phase 🛒 → Prospect), trading, or conquering territories that already held resource cards. Controlling a territory doesn't guarantee automatic production.
bs.reason.neutral: neutral territory — conquer it first by moving an army here
bs.noFleet: No fleet in this sea zone.
bs.legendText: ⚓ = fleets (ships) · 🪖 = embarked armies · cap = occupancy/capacity ({cap} per ship).
bs.embarkHint: To embark/disembark troops, enter the Move phase 🚀.
bs.buildMissing: Missing: {list}. Sell resources or take a loan before building.
bs.noArmyArea: No territory of yours available to build.
bs.noPort: No port under your control — conquer a territory with a port to build fleets.
bs.tapBuildArmy: Tap a highlighted territory on the map to build.
bs.tapBuildNavy: Tap a highlighted sea zone on the map to build.
bs.paidSet: Paid set: {n} unit(s) with no supply cost.
bs.tapMove: Tap a territory (army) or sea zone (fleet) highlighted on the map.
bs.moveNeutralTitle: Neutral territory — moving here conquers it
bs.noCoastalValid: No valid coastal territory (occupied by enemy or destroyed).
bs.tapAttack: Tap a highlighted territory (2+ armies) or sea zone (fleets) on the map.
bs.attackCost: Cost: 1🌾 1🛢️ 1⛏️ per battle · Attacker needs 2+ armies
bs.bombardTitle: Naval bombardment: deals casualties, but doesn't conquer the territory
bs.bombardNote: Bombardment: deals casualties on land, but doesn't conquer (disembark an army to occupy).
map.armyOwnOnly: You can only build an army on your own territory (controlled and not destroyed).
map.fleetInSea: Fleets are built at sea. Tap a highlighted sea zone (adjacent to a port you own).
map.fleetNearPort: A fleet can only be built in a sea adjacent to a port you control.
map.armyOnLand: Armies go on land. Tap a highlighted territory.
